commands.name: ml --b
commands.description: Backup MySQL schemas using configured server credentials.
params: schema (optional, use all for all schemas)
commands.expectedResult: Creates schema SQL dumps under C:\ML CLI\Backup\BACKUP_MM-DD-YY\<schema>.

tutorial.whenToUse: When you need on-demand backups for one schema or all schemas.
tutorial.scenario: Typical database workflow using ml --b.
steps: Run: ml --b
Review output and confirm expected result: Creates schema SQL dumps under C:\ML CLI\Backup\BACKUP_MM-DD-YY\<schema>.
If it fails, apply the recommended fix from Error Handling then retry.

commands.name: ml --c
commands.description: Check local CLI version against remote GitHub version.
commands.expectedResult: Displays whether a newer version is available.

tutorial.whenToUse: When maintaining CLI consistency across machines.

steps: Run: ml --c
Review output and confirm expected result: Displays whether a newer version is available.
If it fails, apply the recommended fix from Error Handling then retry.

firstTimeSetup.explanation: Verify CLI is installed and available in PATH.
firstTimeSetup.expectedResult: Current version is printed, e.g. ML CLI version X.Y.Z.

firstTimeSetup.explanation: Jump directly to C:\xampp\htdocs before creating projects.

firstTimeSetup.expectedResult: Project structure and starter files are created.

firstTimeSetup.explanation: Enter your new project directory and optionally open VS Code.
firstTimeSetup.expectedResult: Terminal is inside your project folder.

firstTimeSetup.explanation: Accept VS Code prompt when asked by nav helper.
Keep using CLI from the project directory.
firstTimeSetup.expectedResult: You are ready for checks and serve flow.

firstTimeSetup.explanation: Run this only if test fails to install missing schema.
Open project in browser and begin development.

generalUsage.expectedResult: Current directory is ready for project operations.

generalUsage.explanation: Create or scaffold your working app.
Preview the app in browser and continue coding.

commonMistakes: Running commands outside C:\xampp\htdocs project context.
Skipping ml test userdb before first run.
Ignoring version checks and using outdated CLI binaries.

scenarios.title: Creating your first project
steps: ml --v
ml nav
ml create my-app
ml nav --my-app
ml serve

scenarios.title: Configuring and running schema backups
steps: ml create --config
ml --b
ml --b userdb
ml --b all

scenarios.title: Fixing database connection issues
steps: ml test userdb
ml add userdb
ml test userdb

scenarios.title: Starting development session
steps: ml --c
ml update (if needed)
ml nav --my-app
ml serve

scenarios.title: Recovering from setup errors
steps: ml --v
ml --c
ml update
ml test userdb

errors.meaning: CLI not installed in PATH or shell session is stale.
errors.fix: Re-run installer, then open a new terminal and run ml --v.

errors.meaning: Network issue or blocked GitHub endpoint.
errors.fix: Check internet, proxy, and retry. Then run ml --c to validate connectivity.

errors.meaning: Schema or credentials are not ready.
errors.fix: Run ml add userdb then retry ml test userdb.

errors.meaning: Backup command ran before DB backup config was created.
errors.fix: Run ml create --config then retry ml --b.

errors.meaning: mysqldump is not installed or not detected from PATH/XAMPP.
errors.fix: Install MySQL client tools or set mysqldumpPath in ml create --config.

faq.q: How often should I run ml --c?
faq.a: Run it at the start of each dev session.

faq.q: Do I need to run ml update every time?
faq.a: Only when a newer version is reported.

faq.q: What is the fastest start command sequence?
